title: US Military Buildup Described as Largest Since 2003
detail: Defense analysts and multiple outlets describe the accelerating US military buildup in the Middle East as the largest since the 2003 Iraq invasion. Assets now deploying include the USS Abraham Lincoln CSG, 35+ F-15E Strike Eagles, additional F-35A squadrons, B-1B Lancers rotating through Diego Garcia, and Army Patriot missile batteries. Total force exceeds 45,000 personnel in the CENTCOM AOR above normal levels.

title: Iran Reveals Underground Missile Base Footage
detail: Iranian state television broadcasts footage of what it claims is a previously secret underground missile base, showing rows of ballistic missiles on mobile launchers. IRGC Aerospace Force commander General Amir Ali Hajizadeh warns that 'hundreds of missiles are aimed at every American base within 2,000 kilometers.' Western analysts assess the footage shows a mix of Emad and Ghadr-110 medium-range ballistic missiles.

title: Oil Prices Spike on Escalation Fears
detail: Brent crude surges above $92 per barrel, its highest level in 18 months, as markets price in the risk of military confrontation near the Strait of Hormuz. Insurance rates for tankers transiting the Persian Gulf increase by 300%. Several major shipping companies announce diversions away from the strait. The spike disproportionately impacts oil-importing nations in Asia and Europe.
